title: 10 Herbal Creams For Irritable Bowel Syndrome
intro_desc: Herbal creams and teas can be a helpful addition to your treatment plan if you have irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). IBS is a condition that affects the large intestine, causing symptoms like abdominal pain, bloating, and changes in bowel movements. Herbal teas, in particular, can help to soothe and calm the digestive system, providing relief from IBS symptoms.  One reason why herbal teas can be beneficial is that they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, is a great example of an herb that has anti-inflammatory properties. It contains a compound called curcumin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of IBS.  Another herb that can be helpful for IBS is Zingiber officinale, or ginger. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to soothe the digestive system and reduce nausea and pain. It's also a natural antacid, which can help to neutralize stomach acid and provide relief from heartburn and acid reflux.  Fennel, or Foeniculum vulgare, is another herb that can be beneficial for IBS. It has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, and its oils can help to relax the muscles in the digestive system and promote regular bowel movements.  When you use herbal teas or creams to help manage your IBS symptoms, you may notice a range of benefits. You may experience reduced abdominal pain and discomfort, improved digestion, and a reduction in bloating and gas. You may also feel more confident and in control of your symptoms, which can have a big impact on your mental health and well-being.  Using herbal creams and teas can also be a natural and non-invasive way to manage your IBS symptoms. You can add them to your daily routine, along with other treatments and lifestyle changes, to help alleviate your symptoms and improve your overall quality of life.
intro_image_alt: herbal creams for irritable bowel syndrome

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a high concentration of curcuminoids, a group of bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory properties. The most active constituent is curcumin, a natural polyphenol that has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, which are common issues associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Curcumin also has a direct effect on the gut microbiome, promoting the growth of beneficial bacteria and reducing the presence of pathogenic bacteria, which can contribute to IBS symptoms. Additionally, curcumin has been found to improve gut motility and reduce inflammation in the intestinal lining, helping to alleviate symptoms such as bloating, abdominal pain, and changes in bowel movements. Curcumin's bioavailability is enhanced in topical creams, allowing it to directly target the affected areas and provide rapid relief from IBS symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for irritable bowel syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of Turmeric powder in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add the Turmeric powder to the melted mixture and stir well. Continue heating for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool and solidify. Your Curcuma longa cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains bioactive constituents like gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, a common issue in irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Gingerols and shogaols also have spasmolytic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms. Additionally, the antioxidants present in Zingiber officinale creams, such as gingerol and shogaol, help protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and damage. By reducing inflammation and muscle spasms, Zingiber officinale creams may help alleviate symptoms of IBS, such as abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for irritable bowel syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of distilled water and 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the coconut oil over low heat and add 1/2 cup of beeswax. Stir until melted.
Add 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale powder, 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the saucepan. Stir well.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 10-15 minutes. Then, whisk the mixture until it thickens.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before use. Store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams contains a compound called trans-anethole, a key active constituent that has anti-inflammatory properties. This property helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, a common issue for people with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Another important constituent, limonene, has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of IBS such as cramping and constipation. The carminative properties of Foeniculum vulgare, which help to expel gas from the digestive system, can also provide relief for IBS sufferers. The combination of these properties makes Foeniculum vulgare creams a potentially effective treatment option for IBS symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for irritable bowel syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds and 2 cups of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until it reaches 120°F to 140°F.
Add the dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 to 3 hours, or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, discarding the seeds.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the strained mixture and stir until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and 18-alpha-glycyrrhetinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive system. These bioactive constituents also have a protective effect on the gut lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to irritable bowel syndrome. Glycyrrhizin also has a laxative effect, helping to regulate bowel movements and reduce constipation, a common symptom of IBS. The anti-spasmodic properties of glycyrrhizin and other compounds in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can also help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and regulating bowel movements, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help alleviate the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for irritable bowel syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 cups of aloe vera gel, 1 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, and 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder.
Heat the distilled water in a saucepan until it's warm, then add the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids. Combine the strained liquid with aloe vera gel and coconut oil in a large bowl.
Add glycerin to the mixture and stir well. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool.
Once cooled, store the creams in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks. Apply a small amount to the skin as needed for relief from irritable bowel syndrome symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contain aloin, aloe-emodin, and barbaloin, which are all active constituents that help soothe and calm the digestive system. Aloin has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the gut, which is often associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Aloe-emodin has laxative properties that help regulate bowel movements and relieve constipation, a common symptom of IBS. Barbaloin has anti-spasmodic properties that help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and discomfort. By reducing inflammation, regulating bowel movements, and relaxing the digestive tract, aloe barbadensis creams can help alleviate symptoms of IBS.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for irritable bowel syndrome
remedy_procedure: Get 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel from 3-4 Aloe vera leaves. Extract the gel by cutting off the green part and scooping out the clear gel.
Mix 1 cup of Aloe gel with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a bowl. Stir well until combined.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the bowl and mix until melted.
Add 2 tablespoons of shea butter and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the bowl. Mix until combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla creams contains apigenin and luteolin, two bioactive constituents that have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the gut, which is common in irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Chamomile also contains alpha-bisabolol, an anti-oxidant that can protect the gut lining from damage and promote healing. The anti-spasmodic properties of apigenin can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms that are often associated with IBS. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Matricaria chamomilla creams may help alleviate IBS symptoms, providing relief for those who suffer from this condition.
remedies.image_alt: Matricaria chamomilla herbal creams for irritable bowel syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until melted.
Add the dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers to the melted oil mixture and let it infuse for 2 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the flowers.
Add the beeswax and vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir until the beeswax is fully incorporated. Pour the mixture into a tin and let it cool and solidify.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams contains a key compound called quinine, which helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract. This relaxation can ease the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) such as abdominal cramps and spasms. The creams also contain other alkaloids like cinchonine and cinchonidine, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract. Quinine has a natural antispasmodic effect, which helps to calm down the overactive muscles that can cause IBS symptoms. By reducing muscle spasms and inflammation, Cinchona officinalis creams can help to provide relief from IBS symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for irritable bowel syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of dried Cinchona officinalis bark, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 1 tablespoon of shea butter.
Combine the distilled water and dried Cinchona officinalis bark in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, or until the mixture is reduced by half.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids and let the liquid cool.
In a separate saucepan, melt the coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter over low heat. Stir until the mixture is smooth.
Combine the cooled liquid from step 3 with the melted mixture from step 4. Stir until well combined. Pour the mixture into a jar and let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains the essential oil linalool, a well-known anti-inflammatory compound. Linalool helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is a common cause of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) symptoms. The cream also contains linalyl acetate, which has a calming effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, helping to alleviate abdominal pain and discomfort. Additionally, Lavandula angustifolia creams contain apigenin, a flavonoid with antioxidant properties that helps to soothe and protect the digestive tract lining. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Lavandula angustifolia creams can provide relief from IBS symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and abdominal pain.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for irritable bowel syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/2 cup of beeswax.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the flowers.
Combine the infused coconut oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and heat until the beeswax melts, then let it cool and solidify into a cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides that help alleviate irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) symptoms. Flavonoids, specifically silymarin, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the gut, a common cause of IBS. Alkaloids, like carinatine, help protect the gut lining from damage, preventing irritation and discomfort. Glycosides, such as silybin, also have anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to soothing the digestive tract. By reducing inflammation and protecting the gut, Silybum marianum creams can help manage IBS symptoms, providing relief from abdominal pain, bloating, and changes in bowel movements.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal creams for irritable bowel syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/2 cup of beeswax.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Silybum marianum flowers to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
In a separate bowl, mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of the infused coconut oil. Heat the mixture in the double boiler or over the simmering water.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool. Pour the mixture into a container and let it solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Peumus boldus creams contains a range of bioactive constituents, including boldine, alkaloids, and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the gut and protect it from damage. The anti-inflammatory effects of Peumus boldus creams can also help soothe the digestive tract, making it easier for food to pass through and reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. The antioxidant properties of boldine and other flavonoids in Peumus boldus creams can help repair the lining of the gut and prevent damage from free radicals. By reducing inflammation and protecting the gut lining, Peumus boldus creams can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, such as abdominal pain, bloating, and changes in bowel movements.
remedies.image_alt: Peumus boldus herbal creams for irritable bowel syndrome
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of water, 1 cup of olive oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of peppermint oil, 2 tablespoons of melissa oil, and 2 tablespoons of boldine extract.
Melt the beeswax and olive oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the shea butter and stir until melted. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Add the peppermint oil, melissa oil, and boldine extract. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool until solid. Label and store in a cool place.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) symptoms from forming. Some herbs, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive system. Ginger is another herb that can calm digestive issues. These teas may also help regulate bowel movements and ease stress, which can contribute to IBS.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a helpful addition to managing irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). However, it's essential to be mindful of daily consumption. Some herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger can cause stomach upset if taken in excess. Start with small amounts and observe your body's response, gradually increasing the quantity as needed.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with irritable bowel syndrome, but the time it takes to see results varies. Some people notice a difference after a few days, while others may take a week or two to feel the effects. It's best to try a tea for at least a week to see if it helps your symptoms.
faq_time: For irritable bowel syndrome, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning. This is because a cup before breakfast can help calm your digestive system and prepare it for the day's meals. It's also a great way to start your day feeling relaxed and focused.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for irritable bowel syndrome. Some herbs, like peppermint and ginger, can slow down or speed up the absorption of certain medications. This may change how well the medications work or increase the risk of side effects. It's best to talk to your doctor about any herbal teas you're taking.
faq_pregnant: If you have irritable bowel syndrome and are pregnant, it's best to talk to your doctor before drinking herbal teas. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, may be safe in small amounts, but others, like licorice root and pennyroyal, can be problematic. Always check the ingredients and amounts carefully.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a helpful addition to managing irritable bowel syndrome, but it's essential to be cautious when breastfeeding. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are commonly consumed during breastfeeding and may be beneficial. However, others like sage, licorice root, and pennyroyal can pass into breast milk and potentially affect the baby. It's crucial to research and carefully choose safe herbal teas.
title_2: 10 Herbal Creams to Soothe Irritable Bowel Syndrome in Middle Aged
